definition: The 1965-69 guns and butter episode is the closest structural analogue to the current fiscal configuration, with ~60% probability of a fiscal/inflation dominance resolution. Structural parallels include large deficits at full employment and sticky inflation, but current deficit is 2-3x larger, debt/GDP 3x higher, and basis trade/leveraged duration positions did not exist.
evidence: Both generalists converge at ~60-65% probability after novel-regime discounts
Structural parallels: large deficits at full employment, sticky inflation
n=2 prior split-correlation episodes insufficient for statistical inference
Alternative Japan post-1990 path plausible at ~25-30%
open_questions: How do structurally different global capital flows alter the 1965-69 transmission channels?
Is the Japan post-1990 path a viable US outcome given different current account positions?
